title: Codonanthopsis anisophylla
intro: Codonanthopsis anisophylla, also known as Anisophylla, is a plant species belonging to the family Bignoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Anisophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anisophylla is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including inflammation and gastrointestinal disorders.
